item.title: Final Fantasy 14 Is Taking Its Next Giant Step With The Evercold Expansion
item.content:encoded:
Great news for Final Fantasy XIV freaks, but bad news for those of us who are still trying to catch up with the MMORPG’s literally hundreds-of-hours-long story: FFXIV is about to get even longer. At the Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival keynote on Friday, Square Enix shared a teaser trailer for the game’s next major expansion, which is officially called Evercold. It’s slated to release in January 2027. 

Evercold is going to be a pretty big deal. The expansion kicks off a new story arc called the Godless Realms Saga, another detail that was revealed today. Pretty much all of the game’s 13 years of content thus far have fallen under a single saga, so Evercold officially starting a brand new one is a big deal. I think we all assumed this already, but the new saga’s announcement also solidifies the fact that FFXIV is probably going to continue sticking around for a long time rather than get replaced by another MMO sequel. 

The expansion’s teaser trailer is pretty vague, but it does showcase the chilly realm of the Fourth reflection, which, spoiler alert, is basically a new world in an alternate dimension. Square Enix has also been sharing some concept art from the Fourth on Twitter, and it looks really, really sick:

Evercold comes almost two years after the release of Dawntrail, the last major expansion, which was…not super well received. A few incoming patches are set to bridge the gap between Dawntrail and Evercold, starting with Patch 7.5 on April 28th.

Other features coming to FFXIV soon are two new mysteriously unnamed jobs (one tank, one physical ranged DPS), same-region player matching, and better character creation options. The Fan Fest crowd screamed pretty ravenously when director Yoshi-P announced this last one in the livestream, although he hilariously clarified through his translator that there’s “no butt sliders…yet.”

Every time a new FFXIV expansion drops, I always jump back into the game in a sisyphean attempt to finally catch up with the story. However, I always just end up fishing instead. I’m still excited to do this goofy little song and dance in January, though, and I am curious to see what new content (and fish) Evercold brings to the MMORPG. Maybe one day I’ll get far enough to actually see it myself.



https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-expansion-ff14-trailer-release-2000690538

item.title: South Korean PM Calls Crimson Desert Turning Point for K-Gaming
item.content:encoded:
South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok has taken to social media to congratulate Pearl Abyss on the success of Crimson Desert. The game recently surpassed 5 million sales, making it the fastest console game in Korean history to reach that milestone.

On April 15, Pearl Abyss announced that Crimson Desert had achieved the milestone in under a month. In just 26 days, the open-world adventure game set a record for all games ever developed in Korea. The game currently has over 91,000 concurrent players at the time of this writing and maintains a steady stream of players, despite reaching its peak of over 276,000 players on launch day.

South Korean Prime Minister Praises Crimson Desert After 5M Sales Milestone

In celebration of this milestone, Pearl Abyss was congratulated by South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok. In a tweet written on April 24, the Prime Minister called the Milestone a crucial Turning Point that demonstrates the domestic game industry in Korea can expand across more platforms. It’s also stated that the government takes responsibility and provides support to ensure the industry’s future success as a pillar of Korean content.

The full statement can be read below:

Achieved 5 million sales in the shortest time in Korean console game history, just 26 days after release.

We sincerely congratulate the achievement of Red Desert, which has elevated the status of the Korean game industry on the global stage.

A living game world crafted entirely with their own technology from start to finish, graphics akin to photorealism, and proactive communication that captured the hearts of gamers worldwide. By naturally incorporating Korean elements like taekwondo and Korean cuisine, it has opened a new chapter in K-content.

This accomplishment serves as a crucial turning point, demonstrating that the domestic game industry can expand and leap forward across diverse platforms, including consoles.

The government will also take responsibility and provide active support. We will create an environment where K-games can shine as a pillar of K-content.

Once again, we congratulate Pearl Abyss on achieving 5 million sales of Red Desert.

For more on Crimson Desert on Insider Gaming, check out the preview of upcoming features and content recently given by the developers.

How do you feel about Crimson Desert so far? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
